channel.title: The Mutapa Times
channel.description: Business and intelligence newspaper delivering curated Zimbabwean news from foreign press for the diaspora.

item.title: Powertel and Paratus Zimbabwe switch on new digital highway
item.description: Powertel and Paratus Zimbabwe switch on new digital highway — via ITWeb Africa #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews

item.title: Mnangagwa Makes Big Military Appointment
item.description: By A Correspondent | President Emmerson Mnangagwa has made another strategic security move by promoting Brigadier General Fidelis Mhonda to the... Continue reading "Mnangagwa Makes Big Military Appointment" (via Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe News

item.title: Zimbabwe Eyes Sex Offenders Register But Government Flags Tough Questions
item.description: Zimbabwe Moves To Establish Sex Offenders Register As Government Weighs Privacy Concerns Zimbabwe is moving to establish a sex offenders register as the Government steps up efforts to curb sexual offences. However, officials say privacy, human dignity and constitutional rights are among the major issues that must be carefully considered before the proposed system takes [...] The post Zimbabwe Eyes Sex Offenders Register But Government Flags Tough Questions appeared first on iHarare News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Iharare News

item.title: Dramatic Courtroom Twist: SDA Evangelist Sam Marufu Walks Free Leaving Three Humiliated Pastors Wives Devastated
item.description: The Digital Pulpit: Cyberbullying Allegations Expose Deep Rifts Within Zimbabwe’s SDA Church In a case that has gripped the nation and exposed the darker side of social media within religious circles, prominent Seventh-Day Adventist (SDA) Evangelist Sam Marufu walked out of the Harare Magistrates Court a free man yesterday. The dramatic turn of events came [...] The post Dramatic Courtroom Twist: SDA Evangelist Sam Marufu Walks Free Leaving Three Humiliated Pastors Wives Devastated first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: TJ Maguranyanga discovering NFL talent in Zimbabwe
item.description: IN a bid to unearth talent, NFL player Thabani ‘T.J’ Maguranyanga hosted a player-led clinic at Harare Sports Club on Thursday. Maguranyanga, who is one of the few Zimbabwean players to have featured in the NFL, is on a drive to discover talent for a sport that is rarely played in the country. The lanky [...] The post TJ Maguranyanga discovering NFL talent in Zimbabwe appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Hardrock FC signs Warriors striker Prince Dube after Young Africans exit
item.description: Hardrock FC have signed Warriors striker Prince Dube following his departure from Tanzania’s Young Africans. The experienced forward became a free agent after his contract with Young Africans expired on Wednesday. Dube had attracted interest from several clubs, including Scottland FC and South African giants Kaizer Chiefs, before opting to join the ambitious Kwekwe-based side. [...] The post Hardrock FC signs Warriors striker Prince Dube after Young Africans exit appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: War Vet files urgent High Court application over CAB 3 as Chivayo’s cash and cars ‘bribes’ backfire
item.description: The Constitution of Zimbabwe: A Document Under Siege by ‘Cash and Keys’ HARARE – In the quiet corridors of the High Court, a legal battle has erupted that threatens to derail the most significant constitutional overhaul in Zimbabwe’s recent history. Reuben Zulu, a veteran of the liberation struggle, has stepped forward with an urgent application [...] The post War Vet files urgent High Court application over CAB 3 as Chivayo’s cash and cars ‘bribes’ backfire first appeared on My Zimbabwe News. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: My Zimbabwe News

item.title: War veteran seeks urgent High Court order to stop CAB3 , argues Chivayo’s gifts tainted Parliamentary vote @wicknellchivayo
item.description: A war veteran has filed an urgent High Court application seeking to stop the certification and further processing of the Constitution of Zimbabwe Amendment Bill No.3 (CAB3), arguing that the parliamentary vote that passed the proposed law was unlawfully tainted by gifts received from controversial businessman Wicknell Chivayo. Reuben Zulu filed the urgent chamber application [...] The post War veteran seeks urgent High Court order to stop CAB3, argues Chivayo’s gifts tainted Parliamentary vote appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: SA crisis: 56 000 troop back home @zimsituation
item.description: Source: SA crisis: 56 000 troop back home – herald Zimbabwean returnees are being processed at the Government-run reception and support centre in Beitbridge. Most are getting ready to leave for their various destinations. – Picture: Edward Zvemisha. Thupeyo Muleya-Beitbridge Bureau MORE than 56 000 Zimbabweans have returned home through Beitbridge Border Post since late [...] The post SA crisis: 56 000 troop back home appeared first on Zimbabwe Situation. #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwe Situation

item.title: Zimbabweans, Malawians arrested as City of Joburg targets illegal occupation of Sandton properties
item.description: IOL Johannesburg authorities launched an operation targeting properties allegedly linked to illegal occupation and immigration violations in Sandton, resulting into over 10 arrests and investigations. Speaking during an inspection outside a property at 41B Adrien Street, Johannesburg MMC for Transport Kenny Kunene said multiple city departments and law enforcement agencies participated in the operation, including [...] The post Zimbabweans, Malawians arrested as City of Joburg targets illegal occupation of Sandton properties appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Over 35,000 migrants processed through Beitbridge, says South Africa’s BMA
item.description: IOL The acting commissioner of the Border Management Authority (BMA), Major General David Chilembe, says more than 35,000 foreign nationals have left South Africa through the Beitbridge Border Post in Limpopo. He provided an update on South Africa’s repatriation programme on Thursday. Chilembe was focusing on the Beitbridge Port of Entry in Musina, where most [...] The post Over 35,000 migrants processed through Beitbridge, says South Africa’s BMA appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Police officer’s disciplinary conviction quashed after High Court finds trial was unfair @PoliceZimbabwe
item.description: The High Court has overturned the disciplinary conviction of a Zimbabwe Republic Police constable, ruling that he was denied a fair hearing after prosecutors repeatedly changed the factual basis of the case against him during the trial. Justice Joel Mambara set aside the conviction and sentence of Constable Edson Chitembetembe, finding that the disciplinary proceedings [...] The post Police officer’s disciplinary conviction quashed after High Court finds trial was unfair appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: Social media takes a jibe at anti-immigrant protesters as three Zimbabweans join Ramaphosa at Joburg AI Summit @CyrilRamaphosa
item.description: Social media users in Zimbabwe and beyond have seized on a video of three prominent Zimbabwean business leaders appearing alongside South African President Cyril Ramaphosa at the Google Cloud Summit in Johannesburg, taking a jibe at anti-immigrant protests taking place across South Africa. Videos from Wednesday’s summit showed Ramaphosa walking with Econet founder Strive Masiyiwa, [...] The post Social media takes a jibe at anti-immigrant protesters as three Zimbabweans join Ramaphosa at Joburg AI Summit appeared first on NewZimbabwe.com. (via The Zimbabwe News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: The Zimbabwe News

item.title: War veteran asks court to halt CAB3, says constitution ‘cannot be amended by cash and keys’ @wicknellchivayo
item.description: Businessman Wicknell Chivayo accused of running a bribe-for-votes scheme The post War veteran asks court to halt CAB3, says constitution ‘cannot be amended by cash and keys’ appeared first on Zimbabwe News Now. (via Zimbabwean News) #Zimbabwe #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Zimbabwean News

item.title: Quiet Money, Deep Roots: How the Oak Foundation Backs Zimbabwe’s Communities
item.description: The Geneva-based Oak Foundation has funded Zimbabwean causes since 1983. In 2025 its Zimbabwe programme made USD 7.9 million in grants, backing palliative care, landmine clearance, regenerative farming and care for children who have lost parents. #Zimbabwe #Policy #ZimbabwePolicy #ZimbabweNews
item.dc:creator: Tapiwa Hillary Phillipo
